subject: Product Management
title: Samsung Galaxy Note 7
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Samsung Galaxy Note 7.

hook.text: Samsung's Galaxy Note 7 was recalled twice and banned from all flights worldwide because it kept catching fire.

buildup.text: Samsung rushed the Note 7 to beat the iPhone 7 launch, compressing the testing timeline to gain a two-week advantage.

discovery.text: The battery was too tightly packed inside the case. Under pressure, it short-circuited and ignited.

twist.text: Samsung's first recall fixed nothing — the replacement batteries had a different manufacturing defect. Phones kept burning.

climax.text: The total recall cost Samsung $5.3 billion and nearly destroyed the Galaxy brand. Rushing to market created a safety crisis.

punchline.text: Shipping two weeks early cost five billion dollars.

quiz.question: What caused the Samsung Galaxy Note 7 fires?
options: Rushed timelines compressed testing — the battery was too tightly packed
Users were charging the phones incorrectly
Samsung used cheap materials to reduce costs
